sentences.text: Hey, Jim, how are you?
Hey, Nathan, good, how are you?
Doing well.
Doing well, it's Friday, so I definitely can't complain.
Friday afternoon, huh?
Oh, yeah.
You got any plans for the weekend?
No, it's gonna be a quiet weekend for us.
Yeah.
Okay.
Hey, sometimes that's always… that's… sometimes that's good.
Yeah.
I sometimes look forward to the, the weekends where I have nothing planned so I can just kind of relax, but….
Yep, yeah, that's kind of where we're at.
Oh, yeah.
So, you're… you're up in PA, right?
Yep, we're, we're based out of Fort Washington, which is, … Right outside of Philadelphia.
Yeah, yeah, okay.
Have you always lived up in PA?
Yeah, I grew up in, like, the Pittsburgh area, and then, after college, moved to Philadelphia area, so I've always been in PA.
Okay, are you a Steelers and Pirates fan?
I am.
I'm a Steelers fan.
Pirates?
I try to be, but….
It's, … it's tough.
I really like Paul Skeens.
Yeah, he's good.
I, I'm a Braves fan.
I was really hoping that we got him, but … we had too good of a year before he came out.
Yeah, yup.
college.
So… Awesome, man.
Well, thank you for hopping on.
I know you're super busy, so I'll try not to keep too much of your time.

… I got… I have a couple of questions for you about, like, your portfolio, what you're seeing up in your end of the world, and then, I'm sure you want to know more details about us and everything that we can offer, so….
Yeah, and I've had a few… I've had a few conversations with, with Rentable in the past, but it's probably been a good year and a half since I've, I think we chatted in the past, actually.
We did.
Yeah, so I just kind of want to learn, you know, where you're at, what your solutions are.
For us, we're… We've had quite a bit of changes in the past year, where in the past we self-managed our communities.
Now we have, third-party managers managing our entire portfolio.
With that being said, we're very involved in… Standardizing our technology and our digital marketing across our portfolio.
Yeah.
But that is, … That's one of the bigger changes we've had over the past year.
Gotcha.
I gotta ask, what, what made you, third-party everything out?
It, it, you know, our business model changed.
We're, in the past, we would hold a lot of our communities.
Now our goal is to develop, lease them up, and then sell them.
So, it's just a different strategy, and… The third-party management to kind of fit that… that model better.
Yeah, no, that makes sense.
A lot of… less overhead on your end, and then probably a little less stress, too.
Yep, exactly.
Yep.
So your process, you develop them, and as soon as they're developed, you have a third-party manager in place.
I saw Bizuto.
Yeah, we work with Bizuto, Graystar, some of the big ones, Willowbridge, RPM, yeah.
They take over everything, you let them be responsible for leasing it up, and then as soon as it's leased up, boom, you sell it.

Yep, yeah, but in terms of, like, our marketing and technology, like, we own all of our accounts, like, our Google Ads accounts and everything like that, so… We have that oversight over the performance of our community.
Yeah.
So we don't just, you know, we're involved in the process, though, and we can kind of influence, you know, if we were to, pilot a new technology or a new channel.
Gotcha.
Okay, cool, no, that's fair.
What all do you do besides… I know you're doing Apartments.
com for a lot of your assets, what else do you do for the advertising side?
We do the, traditional paid search, paid social, display.
we do Zillow as well, and we've… we've started to use apartment lists a little bit more, now that we have some AI solutions in place to manage leads.
They tend to generate a lot of low-quality leads, so… those are really the three… probably the three big, like, ILSs that we're… We're leveraging right now.
Gotcha.
Are you using, … funnel, or Elise AI?
It is, … it depends on the community, but… We are primarily using Elise, for the most part.
And Entrata's your property management software for the most part.
We, we're actually mostly on Yardi now.
We were in Trada, we have a few communities on Trada, but the bulk is on Yardi at this point.
Okay.
I… I… so every time before a call, I usually, like, scope a property out, check to see what the property management software is.
I must have just gotten lucky and got the one property or so that was on it.
Yeah, we probably have maybe 5 communities on Entrada at this point.
So you must have got one of them.
Yeah.
What are the odds?
Okay, it's cool.
So, mostly you're already using Elise AI now.
I've heard really good things about Elise AI.
Yeah, we're piloting, Elise AI and then another technology, kind of going head-to-head to see… You know, who's the best, so….
Is it Yardi's new thing?
No, it's actually a company called Hylie.
Okay, I've heard of highly.
Yeah, so….
Okay.
Well, we, we do integrate with, … property management software is both the ones you're using.
Elise AI we integrate with.
I believe we integrate with Hylie.
I'm assuming it's probably got a very similar setup to Elise AI and Funnels, so if we can do that, I'm sure we can do those.
Okay, yeah, that's good to know.
So, yeah, other than that, the only other question I have left for you, Jim, is what, how's render traffic, renter demand been, how's occupancy?
It's… it's okay, I think we're getting close to that… the end of peak season.
Usually, right in mid-August is when we start to see a decline in, like, lead volume.
Yeah.
So we're just kind of preparing for that.
… you know, the tail end of the peak season.
Yeah.
But overall, we've been doing pretty well, yeah.
Yeah.
No, no specific market, like, hurting worse than the other markets.
It's interesting, like, you know, some of the… some of the markets, like the ILSs, tend… they don't perform as well.
It's kind of interesting as we start to dive into the data a little bit more, so, like, Texas.
it tends to, like, struggle with ILSs for whatever reason, whereas some of our Northeast communities do better, so… It's all over the place.
Yeah, it's… yep.

Yeah, we've, … the reason I asked you that is because I've heard of a lot of people having issues in Texas and Florida, Georgia being one of the ones that they've brought up, which are all markets that we for some reason, seemed to do really well in.
But yeah, so, okay.
That's… that's all the questions I have, so I'll dive in for you, show you updates, and we'll see where to go from here.
Perfect.
Let's see here… Is this okay?
Yep.
Alright, I'm gonna refresh this, get that… Whatever that was off the screen.
Alright, so, we're still on iOS, first and foremost.
Just like, you know, Apartments.
com or a Zillow.

I already kind of alluded to, we integrate with your property management softwares and CRMs, so anytime you're updating info on your end, it'll pull, it'll reflect on our end, and then anytime leads are sent, we can send them to the CRM or to an online leasing email, or both, whichever you prefer.
So, when we pull in your property, we're gonna list it on our rentable network first, and then from there, we're gonna syndicate it out to several different sites within our partnership network.
We have, I think at this point, the industry's largest network.
We go basically everywhere except for Apartments.
com.
So… And what we're doing is we have an AI tool working on the backend.
So, what it's doing is it's making sure that we're not duplicating your listings.

So, if I just talk portfolio-wide, across, like, 32 properties, I think is what I saw on your site, If you have properties on, like, ApartmentList, for an example, we'll make sure for the 10-odd properties that you have on apartment list, we're not pushing and duplicating, but for the other remaining 22, we can still push and get you there.
Okay, interesting.
So… Gio, were you about to ask a question?
No, I'm trying… I'm just trying to think through, like… so, if you're, if you're syndicating to ApartmentList, for example, how does that differ from… like, like, apartment list… listing?
Is it the….
Yeah, from a direct relationship, so we always let the direct partnerships take present.
We don't want to remove the back-end channel partnership agreements that we have.
But, like, for an example, like, if you were to drop apartment lists down the road, like, we would end up picking up.
But the way the listings look, they look identical to if you were to have that direct partnership.
The main difference is, like.
Apartments.
com, for an example, has, like, a Diamonds.
com, or a Diamond package, and then they have their platinum, their gold, whatever.
We're not gonna syndicate over at, like, a diamond level, but we'll syndicate over at, like, that gold, that middle-upper tier.
So, we won't guarantee that you're on page 1 for… per se, but we could say, you know, 2, 3, 4.
Yeah, that makes sense.
And so, you… You do integrate with Apartments.
com, is that what you're saying?
No, not Apartments.
com.
We do not integrate with that one.
Okay.
Unfortunate.
But….
Okay.
Yeah, I get it.
Okay, that makes sense.
And our main play here with this is filling in the gaps, getting your property the exposure on all the sites that we're not utilizing.
Yep.
So, and then, going off of that, we have an all-encompassing targeted social ad piece now.
I don't think the last time we met that we had this all-inclusive, but basically the way it's working is it's reaching out to renters versus waiting for them to come to you.

So, if somebody were to go on a search engine, this will kind of go hand-in-hand with the PPC and everything that you're running, but anytime somebody were to say, show me apartments in… Billy, next time they got on their Facebook or Instagram feed.
within about 45 seconds of scrolling, we would hit them with a targeted rentable ad that looks like this.
It would have pictures of your individual property and details about it.

So, whether it's the lowest advertised price, if it's a pool, pet-friendly, in-unit laundry, whatever it may be, we'll kind of target that individual, and then… From here, where our social ads really differ from a lot of other companies, is other companies usually do one of two things.
They redirect them to their personal website, and at that point, a renter can see all the properties that they're doing ads for in the area.
Or, they'll redirect them to the specific property web address, which, at that point, you lose track of that renter.
Did they apply?
Did they become a lead?
You don't really know.
With ours, they're all lead generating, so we're gonna keep them here.
They click Learn More, we're gonna have them fill out a form that gives their name, their email, their phone number, and we'll deliver that to you into your CRM.
And I think it's been a while, but we've ran, like, the lead form ads in the past, and there was a lot of, like, low-quality, like, almost, like, bot-like traffic.
Do you run into any of those issues?
Are you talking about just, like, on Facebook ads specifically?
Correct, yep.
Every once in a while, when we used to do Facebook Marketplace, it definitely was like that.
Okay.
with this approach, through the social ads, it hasn't been as much so.
The reason is because we can kind of hone in and make it more… we can ask more questions, basically, to make it more quality leads, versus just, like, name, email, phone number.
We can add more questions, like, when are you looking to move?
Got it.
What room floor plan are you looking for?
What price range are you looking to stay under?
Things of that nature.
To kind of provide you with more detail.

And then, going off of that, the same AI tool I mentioned a minute ago that's making sure we're not duplicating listings, it's also working in the background to make sure we're getting you on the best sites that we can possibly get you on.
So, I'll talk portfolio-wide, and, like, you brought on your entire portfolio.
So it'll say, okay, cool, here's the top 3 sites that you're using for property 1, here's the bottom three.

So that way, instead of us duplicate… er, not duplicating, but trying to beat a dead horse over here and get results out of these sites that haven't produced, we can gear it more towards the top 3 sites that are performing.
And then we can not only look at quantity of traffic, but quality traffic.
Are these leads converting to leases?
Are they converting to tours?
Whatever it may be, so if you're more interested in that quality traffic, we can definitely gauge it more towards quality than just quantity.
This is volume.
Okay.
It's good to know.
Yeah.
So, I'll pause there, though.
What other questions do you have for me?
I think it's pretty straightforward.
I mean, you're essentially just serving ads to people that are in the market for apartments, based off their… their search behavior and their behavior online.
So that… that all makes sense.
And I guess, like, the… I can't really see the ad copy there, but you're just pulling that from the… The information on the listings that you already have, or, like, how does that work?
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
So, here, I'll make it a little bigger.
So, ….
Okay, I see.
So, all the information that we're pulling is from your property management software.
So, photos, same thing.
Information on the property, like, if the property obviously isn't pet-friendly, then we're not going to list this.
But all the… all the ads are gonna be AI-generated, and then… It's going to run through an algorithm to see what pictures are getting the most interaction.

So, like, for this example, if it's a kitchen here, this property may get more results with the kitchen being the first photo, but this property over here could have been better having, like, the pool or maybe a picture of the clubhouse first, and it got more interaction that way.
So, we'll kind of adjust the listings and the postings based off of that as well.
Okay, makes sense.
So… sweet.
Amy, can I get out of there?
There we go.
Cool.
That's… that's runnable.
The only other thing I really have, I guess I have some comps that are in your area that we're working with, like HDC Mid-Atlantic.
We're working with Independence IRT, Independence Realty Realty Trust.
Post Road.
We've got a couple of properties on NDC… with NDC Asset Management, but I don't think they're in Philadelphia.
… Those are some of the bigger partners that we're with.
Up your way.
And how… How does your… see, there's rentable, and then in the past, there were… There was a presentation on, like, your Google, the Google Business Profile platform.
Is that… is that now on the Maven AI side?
Yeah, it's on the Maven AI.
We had to separate things out because that product just grew, and they keep adding more and more to it, so….
F?
So yeah, … That, that product's awesome, though.
Still handles a lot with, … automation and time savings on the Google business profiles, adding in that, pricing availability feed.
There's a reputation piece to it, and then there's also a social AI piece where, it's like postings on the property's actual Facebook and different social pages.
Okay.
So…
Yeah, if that's something you want to hear more about, I can always put you in touch with that.
That product specialist.
Okay.
And I guess, what is the, the, like, the pricing model?
Is it a pay-per-lease, or is it a flat fee?
Like, how does that….
Yeah, that was… that was the next part I was getting into.
We're a monthly fee, pennies to the dollar to an Apartments.
com or Zillow, though.
We require a 5-property minimum when coming on board, but it's only $199 a month per property.
Okay.
And that gets you everything from our rentable network, the partnerships, all the paid social.
There's no additional costs or fees.
It gets you a dedicated success manager, access to our backend dashboard.
Gets you everything.
F.
And what do your… what does your reporting look like?
Is there… I'm assuming there's some sort of dashboard?
Yeah, let me pull that up for ya.
Show it to you, give me a sec.
Can you see this?
Yep.
Awesome, so whenever you log in.
You originally pull up to, like, a listing to see, like, all the different properties that we're working with, and then you can go into analytics.
So, analytics is going to show you the lead volume per property.
It autofills to the past 30 days, so you can see, like, ballpark Apartments, Rocky Hill.
They've got 58 and 43 leads in the past 30 days.
Out of those leads, 13 were email, 45 were call to ballpark.
You can also go here and you can change the timeframe.
So if you want to go back and look at the past 90 days, or year, whatever it may be, you can do that as well.
So in the past 90 days, 1,740 leads, 10 were emails, 138 were calls, which, that is an absurd ratio compared to what we usually see, but… Maybe that's how they prefer it.
… leads, you can go in and actually look at the individual leads.
So you can see on 815 at 1.
14, Tessa Lewis applied to Riverfront Crossing, and this is what they said.
This is their email, so you have that information there.
And you can also go in and listen to calls.
So, Rocky Creek Apartments.
Are apparently not recording the calls for some reason, except for this one.
You can go in, click play, and listen to what the conversation was like.
F.
So… and then we can provide different access, so, like, someone like yourself, Jim, you could have access to all the properties.
If you wanted to give regional managers access, we could separate it to where they're only seeing their individual properties versus all of them as well.
Okay.
And the, the $199, is that just… that's the price, or do you have different tiers?
No, we have, like, a one-tier fits all.
Okay.

So $199 is everything, but I… there's been some, like, some partners of ours, like, say, hey, we're having a lot of success on the social boost, we want to add some additional spin there, and they may do, like, some additional, like, one-off properties with more spin for solely social boosts.
Okay And I guess the ILS and the Social Boost is just blended into that cost?
Yeah, yeah.
We're getting everything, like, so, like, if you were to go, you're probably asking that because you've done social ads yourself, and you know how expensive they are.
We've had, like, a relationship with Facebook for years, so we're getting everything, like, dollar a lead versus, like, the $15, $20 a lead that you're doing right now.
Okay.
I think that's it.
Perfect.
I think we covered everything.
I don't have any other questions on my end.
Okay.
How, I guess, going off of that then, how does this compare to what you're currently doing?
It's, I mean, it's definitely a lot cheaper, for sure.
… I think I need to have a conversation with our marketing managers who have their different regions.
To kind of gauge their interest, I think….
If there's interest, that would be the next step, is looping them into a conversation.
Okay.
So….
If you don't mind, are you able to share that, the deck that you presented today?
Yeah, yeah, I'll send that over to you as soon as we hop off with everything.
Okay, that's perfect.
If you want them on a call, I'm more than happy to hop on a call and kind of walk them through everything as well.
Yep, yeah, so let me… let me have a conversation internally, and then, if there's interest, we can… we can schedule a follow-up with the marketing managers.
Okay, that works.
Is there a good day?
I know, obviously, it's almost the weekend, is there a good day next week for us to follow up with him?
Yeah, you can follow up midweek if you… if you just want to send an email, I think that's easier, and just check in.
Yeah, 20th?
Yep, that works.
I'll do that.
Yeah, and I'll send you over that follow-up email here in the next… give me, like, 5… 5-10 minutes.
Okay, perfect, and I appreciate your time.
Yeah, no problem, Jim.
Thank you, as well for hopping on, and I look forward to potentially working together.
Yep.
Have a good weekend.
You as well.
Bye.
